HAIL TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS DAMAGED RV VENTS IN WINNEMUCCA.

WIND DAMAGE FROM A THUNDERSTORM MICROBURST FLIPPED A MEDIUM SIZE HORSE TRAILER...BENT 1.25 INCH METAL RODS AROUND A HORSE CORRAL...RIPPED SECTIONS OF SIDING FROM TRAILER...SWAMP COOLER WAS THROWN 10 FEET OFF THE ROOF AND DISPLACED CAMPER TRAILER ONE HALF FOOT FROM FOUNDATION. OTHER MINOR DAMAGE WAS REPORTED IN THE TOWN OF MONTELLO AND AREA AROUND MICROBURST. PARTS OF THE SIDING WAS ESTIMATED TO BE THROWN GREATER THAN 50 TO 100 YARDS FROM TRAILER.

WENDOVER AWOS REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 62 MPH.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED 6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING THROUGH STREETS OF WEST WENDOVER. ALSO...MENTIONED MANY STREETS HAVE FLOWING WATER AND/OR STANDING WATER.

EMERGENCY MANAGER AND DUCKWATER HEALTH DEPARTMENT REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE ROAD NORTH OF TOWN THROUGH THE PANCAKE MOUNTAIN RANGE. AT LEAST 10 SPOTS OF WATER CROSSING THE ROADWAY...2 FEET DEEP WATER IN DITCHES...RESERVOIRS OVERFLOWING WITH 4 FEET DEEP WATER...AND NDOT HAD TO REPAIR A SIGNIFICANT WASHOUT. OTHER MINOR WASHOUTS WERE REPORTED...AS WELL AS...6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER IN ONE SECTION OF THE ROAD. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED OFF OF MANAGERS REPORT.
DEPARTMENT OF HIGHWAYS AND LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A FLASH FLOOD OCCURRED ALONG STATE ROUTE 318...MILE MARKER 4 IN WHITE PINE COUNTY. HALF MILE WIDE AREA OF MUD AND WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD.

FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 318...MILE MARKER 6. NDOT REPORTED ONE TO ONE AND A HALF FEET OF WATER...MUD...LARGE PINE TREES...AND OTHER DERIS FLOWING ACROSS THE ROADWAY. ROADWAY WAS IMPASSABLE AND WAS CLEARED WITH A SNOWPLOW. TIME IS BASED OF WHEN NDOT RECIEVED CALL.
MINOR STREET FLOODING ALONG HIGHWAY 93 1 MILE NORTH OF ELY. SPOTTER REPORTS VERY HEAVY RAIN.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTS 1 FOOT DEEP WATER ACROSS CENTER STREET IN ELY. STILL RAINING VERY HARD.
FLOODING ON NEVADA STATE ROUTE 376 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 10 AND 40. EXTENT AND DEPTH OF WATER. REPORT RELAYED BY TONOPAH SHERIFFS OFFICE FROM NDOT.
FLOODING AND MUD ON NEVADA STATE ROUTE 377...SAID TO BE ALMOST IMPASSABLE. REPORT RELAYED BY TONOPAH SHERIFFS OFFICE FROM NDOT.
WATER AND DEBRIS ON MAIN STREET...ONGOING AS OF 930 PM PDT. PORTIONS OF ROAD BLOCKED OFF. REPORT FROM TONOPAH SHERIFFS OFFICE.

FLASH FLOOD RELAYED FROM NDOT ON STATE ROUTE 318...MILE MARKER 3.9 TO 4.3. ONE TO ONE AND A HALF FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD. CREWS WERE ON THE SCENE UNTIL EARLY THIS MORNING...JUST CLEARED THE ROAD A COUPLE OF HOURS AGO.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT FLASH FLOODING WASHED OUT SECTIONS OF GREYSTONE MINE ROAD.
RECEIVED NUMEROUS REPORTS AND PICTURES FROM THE PUBLIC AND NWS EMPLOYEES OF FLASH FLOODING AMONG MANY STREETS WITHIN THE CITY OF ELKO. ESTIMATED 1 TO 2 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS ROADWAYS.

RECEIVED NUMEROUS REPORTS AND PICTURES FROM THE PUBLIC AND NWS EMPLOYEES OF FLASH FLOODING AMONG MANY STREETS WITHIN THE CITY OF ELKO. ESTIMATED 1 TO 2 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS ROADWAYS. ALSO...THE GENERAL PUBLIC REPORTED AT LEAST ONE BASEMENT FLOODED AND SOME GARAGES FLOODED IN ELKO.

NWS EMPLOYEES REPORTED THREE SEPERATE AREAS OF WATER...WHICH FLOWED ACROSS THE ROAD IN LAMOILLE CANYON. THE ROAD ALSO HAD AREAS OF MUD...ROCK...AND DEBRIS. MOST OF THE FLASH FLOODING WAS BELOW THOMAS CAMPGROUND.
NVDOT REPORTS MUD SLIDE CLOSE TO ADOBE SUMMIT NEAR HWY 225 DUE TO HEAVY RAINS.

NWS EMPLOYEES REPORTED THREE SEPERATE AREAS OF WATER...WHICH FLOWED ACROSS THE ROAD IN LAMOILLE CANYON. THE ROAD ALSO HAD AREAS OF MUD...ROCK...AND DEBRIS. MOST OF THE FLASH FLOODING WAS BELOW THOMAS CAMPGROUND. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF OF RADAR AND REPORTS.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED AT LEAST 2 FT OF WATER WITH MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 789 NEAR MILE MARKER 6. CALLED A SECOND TIME, AND REPORTED ROADWAY WAS BEGINNING TO WASH OUT.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED FLOODING AT THE HEALTH DEPARTMENT BUILDING AT 511 DUCKWATER ROAD. FIRE DEPT WAS CALLED OUT TO ASSIST IN CLEARING THE BUILDING.

LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED FLOODING AT THE HEALTH DEPARTMENT BUILDING AT 511 DUCKWATER ROAD. FIRE DEPT WAS CALLED OUT TO ASSIST IN CLEARING THE BUILDING. DEBRIS FROM FLOODING MADE STATE ROUTE 379 IMPASSABLE BETWEEN MILE MARKER 4 AND 6.

NEWSPAPER REPORTS MULTIPLE ROADS COVERED WITH WATER OR WASHED OUT IN AND AROUND BATTLE MOUNTAIN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR AND CONVERSATION WITH PAPER.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED AT LEAST ONE FOOT OF MUD AND DEBRIS WAS PUSHED OUT OF THE ROAD....CONFIRMED BY FOREST SERVICE. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR.
NEWSPAPER RELAYED NUMEROUS REPORTS OF STREET FLOODING AND DEBRIS IN MULTIPLE ROADWAYS IN AND AROUND WINNEMUCCA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF OF REPORT AND RADAR.
